##RRUFFID=R070425
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_Pb_3_(AsO_4_)_3_(OH)
##LOCALITY=Langban, Filipstad, Varmland, Sweden
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Michael Scott
##DESCRIPTION=Aggregates of prismatic colorless crystals, associated with lizardite. NEW MINERAL, OH analogue of hedyphane, perhaps call it hydroxylhedyphane
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R070425
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Pb_3.00_(Ca_1.55_Ba_0.20_Pb_0.10_)_1.85_[(AsO_4_)_2.55_(PO_4_)_0.15_(SiO_4_)_0.17_]_2.87_[(OH)_0.55_Cl_0.37_]_0.92_ ; The darker phase is lizardite.
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.052 b: 10.052 c: 7.2793 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 637 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
